info.name: 360Ghar Property Management (PM) API
info.description:
Postman collection for the **Property Management** flows built on top of the 360Ghar backend.

Base Paths:
- Auth: `/api/v1/auth/*`
- Property Management: `/api/v1/pm/*`
- Public rental applications: `/api/v1/pm/public/*`

## Quick Start
1) Import this collection.
2) Import `docs/postman/property-management-environment.json` (or use your existing env and add the variables).
3) Set `BASE_URL` (example: `http://localhost:8000`).
4) Run **00 - Setup & Auth → Login (Phone+Password)** (or OTP verify).
5) Follow the folders in order (properties → applications → lease → rent/expenses/maintenance/docs/inspections → reports).

## Notes
- MVP is **manual-first**: no payment gateway; rent payments are recorded manually (cash/transfer/check).
- No vendor module: maintenance work orders are handled by Owner/RM.
- Many endpoints have an optional `owner_id` scope for **agent/admin**. Owners should leave `owner_id` empty/disabled.
- File upload requests (`/pm/documents/upload`) require selecting a local file in Postman.

exec: // Optional: clear expired token (heuristic based on token_expiry)

item.name: 00 - Setup & Auth
item.description:
Authenticate and initialize common variables.

Recommended:
- Use Login (phone+password) for local/dev
- Use OTP only if Supabase OTP is configured for your project

After successful auth, `access_token` and `current_user_id` are saved into the active environment.

exec: // Default PM owner id to current user if not already set (useful when logging in as owner)

request.description:
Logs in using Supabase password auth.

Saves:
- `access_token`
- `current_user_id`
- `pm_owner_id` (if unset)

Requests an OTP for phone login.

Requires Supabase OTP configuration.

Verifies OTP and returns a bearer token.

Set `otp` in the environment before running.
Validates the bearer token and returns the current user profile.

exec: console.log('No agents returned. Ensure agents exist in DB and are active/available.');

request.description:
Lists available agents (existing 360Ghar endpoint) and saves `pm_agent_id` from the first result.

If you want a specific agent, pick the id from the response and set `pm_agent_id` manually.

Tenant view: list charges for the logged-in tenant user.

Pre-req:
- Login as tenant (or use a tenant token)
- Lease must have `tenant_user_id` set to this user and be `active`

item.name: Tenant: Payment Intent (I Paid) + optional receipt

request.description:
Tenant flow: mark a charge as paid (manual intent) with optional receipt.

Important:
- This endpoint currently validates a body that includes `charge_id` even though the path also includes it.

Pre-req:
- Login as tenant
- `pm_charge_id` must belong to this tenant user

item.name: 01 - PM Dashboard
item.description: Owner/RM dashboard metrics and activity feed.

request.description: Returns computed dashboard KPIs for the current owner.

Use this when you are logged in as agent/admin.

Requires:
- `pm_owner_id` set to the target owner user id.
Recent activity across rent/maintenance/leases/etc. for the current owner.
Agent/admin scoped activity for a specific owner.

item.name: 02 - RM Assignment (Owner ↔ Agent)
item.description: Relationship Manager assignment. Implementation reuses `users.agent_id` (no separate assignment table).

request.description:
Assigns an RM (agent) to the currently logged-in owner.

Set:
- `pm_agent_id` (agent id) before running.

Note: `owner_user_id` is ignored for owner role.

Admin-only: assign RM for a specific owner.

Requires:
- `pm_owner_id` (owner user id)
- `pm_agent_id` (agent id)
Lists current RM assignment for the logged-in owner.
Admin-only: list assignments. Use `owner_id` to filter.

Updates RM assignment for the given owner.

Owner can only patch their own id. Admin can patch any owner.

To unassign, set `agent_id` to null.

body.raw:
{
  "title": "PM Test Property - Flat 302",
  "description": "Managed rental property for PM flows",
  "property_type": "apartment",
  "purpose": "rent",
  "base_price": 30000,
  "city": "Mumbai",
  "state": "Maharashtra",
  "full_address": "Andheri East, Mumbai",
  "monthly_rent": 30000,
  "security_deposit": 60000
}

request.description:
Creates a managed property for the logged-in owner.

Saves:
- `pm_property_id`

body.raw:
{
  "title": "PM Test Property (Agent) - Flat 401",
  "description": "Managed rental property created by agent/admin",
  "property_type": "apartment",
  "purpose": "rent",
  "base_price": 35000,
  "city": "Bengaluru",
  "state": "Karnataka",
  "full_address": "HSR Layout, Bengaluru",
  "monthly_rent": 35000,
  "security_deposit": 70000
}

request.description:
Agent/admin can create managed properties for a specific owner.

Requires `pm_owner_id`.
Lists managed properties accessible to the current owner.
Agent/admin list properties for a specific owner.

Returns property management detail including `active_lease` (if any).

Requires `pm_property_id`.

Updates PM-related settings (due day, grace, late fee policy).

Requires `pm_property_id`.

item.name: 04 - Applications (Forms + Submissions)
item.description: Create rental application forms, fetch via public slug, submit applications, and approve/reject.

request.description:
Creates an application form for a property.

Requires:
- `pm_property_id`

Saves:
- `pm_application_form_id`
- `pm_application_slug`

Fetch a form by id.

Requires `pm_application_form_id`.

item.name: Public: Get Application Form by Slug

request.description:
Public endpoint for prospects. No auth.

Requires `pm_application_slug`.

Public submission endpoint. No auth.

Requires:
- `pm_application_slug`
- `pm_property_id`

Saves:
- `pm_application_id`

List applications for a property.

Requires `pm_property_id`.

Fetch an application by id.

Requires `pm_application_id`.

Approves the application.

Valid values: `approved` or `rejected`.

Requires `pm_application_id`.

item.name: 05 - Leases
item.description: Create, view, upload signed doc, renew, terminate.

body.raw:
{
  "owner_id": null,
  "property_id": {{pm_property_id}},
  "tenant_user_id": null,
  "tenant_name": "Rohit Sharma",
  "tenant_phone": "+919999999999",
  "tenant_email": "rohit@example.com",
  "status": "active",
  "start_date": "2025-01-01",
  "end_date": "2025-12-31",
  "monthly_rent": 30000,
  "security_deposit": 60000,
  "late_fee_amount": 500,
  "late_fee_percentage": null,
  "grace_period_days": 3,
  "payment_due_day": 5,
  "lease_terms": {
    "notice_period_days": 30
  },
  "special_clauses": "No smoking. No subletting.",
  "lease_document_id": null
}

request.description:
Creates a lease and sets it to `active`.

Requires:
- `pm_property_id`

Saves:
- `pm_lease_id`

Note: For tenant portal access, create/link a real tenant user and set `tenant_user_id`.

Fetch lease by id.

Requires `pm_lease_id`.

Links an uploaded document (PDF) as the signed lease.

Requires:
- `pm_lease_id`
- `pm_lease_document_id` (create via Documents → Upload Lease Agreement)

Renews a lease by creating a new lease record.

If `make_active=true`, it becomes the active lease and updates property pointers.

This request overwrites `pm_lease_id` to the new lease id.
Terminates a lease. If it was the active lease, property pointers are cleared.

item.name: 06 - Rent Ledger (Charges + Payments)
item.description: Generate rent charges (idempotent), list charges with totals, record payments, view payment history.

request.description:
Generates charges for an active lease. This endpoint is idempotent.

Requires:
- `pm_lease_id`

item.name: List Charges (by Lease) + Save first charge_id

request.description:
Lists charges and computes totals.

Saves:
- `pm_charge_id` (first row)

Records a rent payment manually.

Requires:
- `pm_charge_id`

Optional:
- `pm_receipt_document_id` (upload a receipt in Documents first)

Saves:
- `pm_payment_id`

body.raw:
{
  "owner_id": null,
  "property_id": {{pm_property_id}},
  "category": "maintenance",

request.description:
Creates an expense linked to a property.

Requires `pm_property_id`.

Saves `pm_expense_id`.

Updates an expense.

Requires `pm_expense_id`.

item.name: 08 - Maintenance (Requests + Work Orders)
item.description: Tenant/owner submits a request; owner/RM updates work order lifecycle.

body.raw:
{
  "property_id": {{pm_property_id}},
  "category": "plumbing",
  "urgency": "high",
  "title": "Leaking tap",
  "description": "Kitchen tap is leaking continuously",
  "preferred_contact_method": "phone",
  "availability_notes": "Available after 6 PM"
}

request.description:
Creates a maintenance request.

Requires `pm_property_id`.

Saves `pm_maintenance_request_id`.

Owner/RM updates request status, work order status, schedule, and costs.

Requires `pm_maintenance_request_id`.

item.name: 09 - Documents (Vault)
item.description: Upload and manage documents linked to users/properties/leases/maintenance/applications.

request.description:
Uploads a document to Supabase storage and creates a `documents` record.

Action required:
- Select a local PDF file for the `file` field.

Saves:
- `pm_document_id`
- `pm_lease_document_id`
Uploads a receipt/proof document and saves `pm_receipt_document_id` for use in rent payment recording.

Updates a document's title and sharing flags.

Requires `pm_document_id`.

Returns a download URL for the document.

MVP returns `file_url` (public URL).

Creates an inspection checklist.

Requires `pm_lease_id`.

Saves `pm_inspection_id`.

Fetch an inspection checklist.

Requires `pm_inspection_id`.

Links signature documents (if available) and timestamps the sign-off.

Phase 2: capture signatures as docs and pass their document ids here.

item.name: 11 - Tenants (Owner/RM)
item.description:
Tenant directory derived from leases where `tenant_user_id` is set.

To see results here, create a lease with `tenant_user_id` pointing to an actual user record.

request.description:
Agent must pass `owner_id`.

Requires `pm_owner_id`.

Fetch tenant user + leases.

Set `pm_tenant_user_id` to a real user id that is referenced by a lease's `tenant_user_id`.

Agents must also pass `owner_id`.

item.name: 12 - Reports
item.description: Computed reports (rent roll, income, expenses, P&L, occupancy, maintenance).
